review_title: Terrific software with an even better support team behind it!
description: The highlight of using OnSemble has been the team at Passageways. [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] was an absolutely invaluable resource and helped solve virtually every one of our business needs and "wins" we wanted for launch. She was full of ideas for solving problems and made herself very available for assistance. On the support side, [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] has also proved to be a massive help and great resource. Always willing to help and fixed our tickets in a very quick timeframe. The people and team alone make OnSemble a great choice!
author: Burke T.
pros: This product is very easy to get set up, using a mixture of basic HTML and drop-n-drag elements to build pages in minutes. It's definitely feasible to build out a department in minutes and hours, through a pretty user friendly interface. You also don't have to sacrifice a lot of control for the ease of use, as you can easily code in what's needed, or can adjust templates on the fly. It allows a ton of customization as well, which gives your team a lot of options for set up and functionality. There's also a lot of features included in the price, which really raises the value when comparting to similar options.
cons: Our issues mostly have been with some of the specific modules called "islands". Some are more robust than others, and we were not able to replicate everything that we wanted given some restraints of the islands. They also prove to be difficult to work with in a few cases. Calendars, for example, offers some nice features but has some quirks that has prevented us from utilizing it for the whole organization. That being said, these islands get updated and changed over time, so some of our issues could get fixed.

description: Enterprise risk management including audit management, business continuity planning, cybersecurity assessments, risk assessments and vendor management.
author: Ryan T.
pros: Ease of use and integration points between the various software applications.
cons: Lack of custom report writing options available to the administrator.

description: OnSemble has been a great one stop shop resource for our staff. We’ve streamlined several processes and reduced the amount of email received. It’s created a fun environment for our staff.
author: Samantha R.
pros: Continual updates. We’ve been using Passageways for a long time but hadn’t kept up with upgrades. Upon relaunch, we were happy to be able to take advantage of many enhancements that have been provided along the way.
cons: There are things that could be streamlined with building tools and roles/permissions. Sometimes, the site is buggy.

description: Positive, It's a really nice intranet software with a small profile.
author: Jack L.
pros: The simplicity (once the project team gets you on your feet) is much better than alternatives. We switched from Sharepoint because of the administrative overhead.
cons: The lack of features compared to Sharepoint. I would have liked for there to be SQL reports accessible through the intranet like sharepoint.

author: Sean C.
pros: The modules and islands. Not only can you choose your site's functionality, but the options on a single content page allowed us to reconfigure our navigation and provide more useful content per page.
cons: Not much of anything bad to say. The platform works smoothly, is a huge improvement over our previous platform, and the support is spectacular.

description: The customer service has always been amazing. The Team is always there to reply and help as quickly as possible. [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] in particular is an all-star who goes above and beyond what it takes to get stuff done. We're really appreciated having her work on our file.
author: Rebecca S.
pros: I like that you are able to restrict access to different parts of your Portal. This is a wonderful customization feature that allows us to connect with users in different ways.
cons: The back-end can be restrictive when trying to design pages. I also wish that it was easier to move documents around from folder to folder and that the folder icons were larger.

review_title: Easy to use platform and great customer service
description: We have an excellent working relationship with NContracts and their team. They consistently provide us knowledge and solutions to our ever changing environment in the financial industry.
author: Misty W.
pros: NContracts provides services for our HMDA, Fair Lending and Red Lining data. It is easy to import our annual information and review where we have been versus where we want to go. The reporting is comprehensive. We can utilize filters to drill down in findings, as applicable. NContracts gives us valuable insight into what we do versus our peers using dialogue and graphs. We can ask questions at any time with confidence that the customer service will be responsive had helpful.
cons: We are assigned an annual review with an analyst. Sometimes I am unsure which questions I should ask to utilize this built-in with our contract. It would be great to have an annual review with a template of suggested material to cover or learn about.

description: The overall experience has been amazing as indicated above. But the lion share of my praise goes to the Implementation Manager. Without her I highly doubt that our Portal would have launched or without a doubt significantly delayed. Her knowledge base of the Onsemble platform and her attention to detail is astounding. As much as I get praised for creating an immersive Intranet, I know it's partly due to her support. She's an asset to the Passageways Organization...and to our team. The support team is nothing like I have ever experienced. Their response time and follow through is indeed a standard for the industry of IT support, this deserves 100% not 99% satisfaction.
author: Floyd A.
pros: Wow!... I can spend literally hours writing about my extreme satisfaction with the team and the software. It has allowed our organization to experience another level of engagement with our staff. The ease of use and the “drag and drop” design feature has also allowed a team of 2 to be nimble, with the results and appearance of a team of 10. Our Executive leadership team believes transparency is the currency of leadership and the Onsemble platform has afforded them the opportunity to live out this value. From just- in-time information, thoughts on upcoming and/or current changes, future business strategies and recorded quarterly messages. This software has been and a catalyst to our increased employee engagement scores. We've only been 'live' for 6 months!
cons: A missing mobile platform and the non responsiveness to different screens for the desktops version.

review_title: Can be both functional and beautiful, with assistance.
description: [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] really saved us through implementation. There's so much to learn with the portal and she holds all of that knowledge in her head and was gracious enough to share it with us through countless meetings.
author: Tanja H.
pros: I love the abundance of modules that we can use that streamlines processes. We've implemented help desks throughout many departments in our organization which has helped speed up requests and allow the ability to track tickets. And, when it doubt, it's nice to have the PTE island to be able to customize how we wish.
cons: Our organization cares a lot about things looking nice - and unfortunately, the drag & drop functionality doesn't inherently look 'pretty' without a bit of work. We had to do a few workarounds to get the site to be responsive and oftentimes need to pop into the HTML code, which many average users aren't familiar with. Also, the calendar doesn't work well for signing up for events like volunteer opportunities and is extremely difficult to report on. I've already submitted a feature enhancement request for this.

author: Cheryl H.
pros: Modules integrate together to share information. Centralized storage for important information that is easily accessible outside the bank, especially in times of need.
cons: So much capability that it is hard to take advantage of everything it has to offer and use it to its fullest potential.

description: Overall, the software is very user-friendly. It has options to create/build additional setup if needed. Support has always been very quick to respond, are knowledgeable and willing to help. They have webinars to continually learn for free and a good knowledge base to use for lookup. I would recommend this software.
author: Leeann P.
pros: I appreciate the available Dashboard to quickly see where I stand within the software. I enjoy the colorful setup and the system was very easy to learn.
cons: The reports could be a little more dynamic to create and offer all of the fields available but if I ever need a report that I can't create, support is always willing to create for me.

description: OnSemble has given us a mechanism to have more current communication without blasting out ALL emails. The central repository for resources and documents has proven to help keep documents up to date and reduce duplication. The help desk module has streamlined several processes. Overall, we are happy with OnSemble. It has brought us more current with our practices and is helping to get away from using email blasts and server folders for everything. After a year, we are still learning and revising and updating and refreshing. We are looking forward to additional updates to take some of the pain points away from our current version.
author: Melinda W.
pros: We were looking for a one-stop-shop for employee communication, documentation and other processes needing streamlined. OnSemble has provided a great platform for this. While it has not solved all our internal needs, it has provided a valuable, flexible and fun resource. *Central repository for documentation, policies, procedures and forms, etc. *Search function that makes finding documents a snap. *Ability to personalize pages, giving departments flexibility to build to their needs. *The help desk module has been excellent to work with and has been implemented for all but one area. *The supply/inventory module has made supply ordering for branches so much easier and user-friendly. *Building pages, once the basics are down, is pretty easy, even for the least "techy" person. *Customer support is fantastic and they have an online forum where you can collaborate with other OnSemble users. *Ability to create roles where people can be divided into different categories, allowing for confidentiality where and when needed.
cons: *System Permissions module seems to be a bit out of date and does not appear to have upgrades to its functionality. This was a feature we were hoping to use more robustly. *Forms module appeared to be something we could utilize, but does not customize as easily as we had hoped. However, we know other users have used it successfully. *There are periodic bugs that don't seem to make sense or will only be repaired with updates. *There is a risk of creating too many layers of permissions, making it cumbersome and confusing if you do not create a good mapping from the onset. *Implementation seemed a little clunky, but it may have been we did not know the right questions to ask in the beginning.

description: The overall experience with OnSemble and the team has been positive. Our CSM's have been responsive and the tech team grabs our tickets and answers right away. The product is easy to use with relatively little training.
author: Anthony B.
pros: Announcements, calendar and discussions are the most beneficial parts of the software for use. They help with our communication need. As one of the Admins I like the PTE islands and that I can write in HTML to impact the page.
cons: I would like to see improvement to sign ups on the calendar. Currently when limiting events it still allows users to sign up. It would be nice to give them notice (other than email) that it is full. Or create a new island that can be used as a sign up sheet. The framework is also boxy. It would be nice to have other shapes to the islands.

description: My overall experience with NContracts has been superb! It's been a necessary resource for our bank.
author: Chondrea B.
pros: Customer Service is very responsive and the ability to have a one on one consult twice a year with an expert is awesome!
cons: Initially understanding the system and what the information was telling me was overwhelming. However having great customer service made that easier over time.

review_title: Always ask for assistance if needed
description: Overall, Onsemble can deliver what you are looking for. I would not classify them as top tier yet, but if things keep improving then I may change my mind. Tech support is the best system support I have dealt with in my professional career. Their response time is fast and I know anyone that has an issue with the portal appreciates that.
author: Jonathan C.
pros: I was thrown into our portal from day 1 and had to learn how to access a lot of it on my own and by attending the annual Passageways conference. It is safe to say, I feel a lot more comfortable now than I did 2 years ago. Something may break, but tech support does an amazing job of reaching out and resolving it for you. If the problem cannot be resolved through email, they have no issue with hopping on a call with you to resolve it.
cons: We are not on the most updated version right now. However, going forward I feel that the portal could use a more modern look to it. We have run into a couple issues of not being able to do something exactly the same way in Chrome as we can do in Internet Explorer, which is not a huge deal, but can be inconvenient for staff at times.

review_title: Ncontracts supports our business woth robust features and support
author: Chip C.
pros: The platform is easy to configure while providing some degree of flexibility to allow us to adapt to changes or custom fields needed for reporting. The integration between modules is very good which prevents our team from re-keying redundant information. Their support and training is also far superior than most other technology providers.
cons: Initial configuration, while not difficult, does take some time to enter all of the necessary information. While much of the informational fields are not required, they are needed if you truly want the full benefit of the product.

review_title: I'm here because I didn't win.
author: Anissa L.
pros: I really enjoyed meeting the team that was sent to the FIRMA conference.
cons: I have no dislikes about this product. Based on conversations, I think i would have few dislikes.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Bethany Z.
pros: I think the best selling point for this software is the support, because it doesn't matter what something looks like if it doesn't work. The OnSemble support team is absolutely excellent. Their response times are usually same day, and everyone is friendly and helpful above and beyond the minimum to get the customer off the phone and out of their hair. When you pair that wonderful support experience with the functionality and flexibility of their actual software product, no other intranet I've experienced compares. Our portal allows us to create a website-like experience for our users, increasing engagement and giving us a better ROI than other options I've used in the past.
cons: This is something that their developers are working to upgrade, but many of the modules have aspects that look a little outdated design-wise or don't have the ability to customize colors and fonts the way I'd like in an ideal world. However, this is very minimal, and as I said, something their team is working to correct.

author: jacqueline L.
pros: Support and Account Managers/Relationship managers are very responsive. Fairly easy to navigate the system.
cons: Some of the programs don't "talk" to each other as smoothly as we'd like, but they are working on those improvements.

description: We've come leaps and bounds in our communications since using OnSemble. Our News Feed and announcement islands have helped consolidate and streamline communications. The search feature and document listings make it easy for staff to find what they are looking for, and the Help Desks have been extremely beneficial.
author: Ellen H.
pros: Our organization loves the set up and functionality. The user set-up, workgroup access, and island options have really helped us to increase our communication between locations and to engage our employees. What I love most is that our portal gives a landing page for our staff to come together, share information, resources, and feel connected.
cons: I think some of the islands and features are still a little clunky and some of the designs look a little out-dated. It would be great of the site was more responsive to different devices, and if there was a mobile option for both iphone and Android. We'd also like to see improvements made for document sorting/display on list islands. It would be great if there were options to organize accordion drop-down menus for files, and if you could choose how uploaded documents would be displayed.

description: Our employees now say they feel like they have a state-of-the-art intranet. They started using it so easily from day 1.
author: Amber H.
pros: Honestly, intranet software is funky. It's all funky. Some of it's funkier than others, and I would say OnSemble is an acceptable level of funkiness. What REALLY makes the difference is your Customer Support Manager and the tech support team. They are good, kind people who truly want to make the product work for you. They work so hard to make you happy and they are a joy to go through this beast of a project with. That's why I would choose OnSemble again. This is my 4th intranet relaunch project, so I know what I'm talking about.
cons: Some modules are built in a way that doesn't make sense from a user or author perspective, and you have to do some workarounds to get them to work "normal." I made a bunch of feature requests and am confident Passageways is working to improve their product.

description: I've been working with OnSemble for about 4.5 years. I've learned a lot about development and collaboration and have seen the platform change and improve year after year. Passageways is dedicated to providing the best product and is always making changes to their software to support the needs of their clients.
author: Erin M.
pros: This software allows us to create and maintain an engaging usable intranet portal without hiring outside programmers or spending excessive time trying to figure out what we are doing. The end result is a professional, functional and aesthetically stunning intranet that is easy to use.
cons: Because we house it on our internal server, some changes require a phone call to customer support and scheduling access to our server, however that is not the norm if you're not hosting it yourself.

review_title: Almost everything you need in an intranet
description: We have most of our company documents centrally located now, and departments are able to manage their own content, rather than channeling all requests through a single point of contact.
author: Andrew B.
pros: There are several modules that are available for you to make your intranet as simple - or complex - as you need it to be. The portal flexibility is helpful.
cons: Some of the modules may be too simple, or not fully customizable in the ways you expect - but Passageways Support and Consulting are available to help craft solutions that fit your needs.

review_title: Easy to use product, with exceptional customer support!
description: The OnSemble software and team have been a very large part of my job day in and day out. From the software itself, to each and every OnSemble employee, I couldn't be happier!
author: Shane K.
pros: This software is very easy to use, and for those areas where I may be unfamiliar, OnSemble provides ample support documentation and videos.
cons: If I had to list one thing, it would be that some of the features were difficult for me to figure out at first (basic portal page editing). However, I was then introduced to the OnSemble support site, where I was able to find answers to my questions very quickly and efficiently!

description: I would recommend Ncontracts to anyone. Nvendor and Ncontracts are very easy to use and can utilized as much or as little as you like and still be extremely effective. We love having all of our vendor information and due diligence in one easy place that all contract administrators have access to. Any vendor updates flow over to our Business Continuity Plan in Ncontinuity, so our BCP always has the most recent vendor contact information. We upload an employee data report monthly, so the BCP is always current with our call lists and crisis teams. During the past year with COVID and wildfires in CA, Ncontinuity has been invaluable.
author: Cheree P.
pros: Our bank has used Ncontracts for over eight years. We absolutely love the executive critical vendor summaries in Nvendor, most especially the SOC Report summaries. We start with those summaries and add our CUEC responses. Auditors and regulators love them!
cons: Our least favorite feature of Ncontinuity is the difficulty of updating our information and then printing the final Business Continuity Plan. It has taken a lot of practice, but we are getting there.
